steps.title: Put the equation in standard form
steps.content:
This is a quadratic equation, so use standard form before applying the quadratic formula. Subtract $43$ from both sides:
$$
x^2+7x-43=0
$$

steps.title: Use the quadratic formula
steps.content:
Here $a=1$, $b=7$, and $c=-43$. Substitute these values and simplify the expression under the radical:
$$
x=\frac{-7\pm\sqrt{7^2-4(1)(-43)}}{2(1)}
$$
$$
x=\frac{-7\pm\sqrt{221}}{2}
$$

steps.title: Find the requested sum
steps.content: Comparing with the given form shows that $m=-7$ and $q=221$. Add those two values: $$ m+q=-7+221=214 $$ The answer is 214.
